sriov.description: obsolete - replaced by sriov-network-operator; dummy unit for backward compatibility

okd_adaptation.enable_pattern: sriov.enabled: true in env values triggers sriov-network-operator via backward-compat expression
okd_adaptation.note: can also directly enable sriov-network-operator instead

sriov-crd.description: installs CRDs for SR-IOV Network operator
resources: CRDs for SriovNetworkNodePolicy, SriovNetwork, SriovNetworkNodeState, etc.

okd_adaptation.reason: OKD ships multus built-in; multus-ready unit is disabled so dependency must be removed to avoid blocking

sriov-init.description: creates sriov requirements - creates the cattle-sriov-system namespace with privileged PSS labels

kyverno_policies: restart-sriov-operator-webhook-on-cert-change: restarts operator-webhook DaemonSet when cert Secret changes
inject-restart-annotation-into-operator-webhook-daemonset: injects cert hash annotation on DaemonSet create/update
sort-sriov-device-plugin-node-selectors: sorts nodeSelector list to prevent unnecessary rollouts

sriov-network-operator.description: installs SR-IOV operator from SUSE Edge charts (same operator on all distros)

sriov-resources.description: configures SRIOV resources (VFs) based on sriov.node_policies values

ingress-nginx-init.description: creates the default certificate for the ingress-nginx controller
gaps_okd: not needed - OKD uses openshift-default ingress class via built-in Router
not needed - OKD ships built-in Router (HAProxy-based) with IngressClass openshift-default
all downstream units use conditional depends_on so they skip ingress-nginx when disabled

okd_adaptation.default_strategy: disable - OKD built-in Router replaces this unit entirely
okd_adaptation.alternative_strategy: coexist - enable ingress-nginx alongside OpenShift Router, nginx handles Sylva Ingress objects
okd_adaptation.ingressClassName_switch: cabpoa -> openshift-default (default), override _internal.ingressClassName: nginx for coexistence
okd_adaptation.scc_wiring: conditional _component in openshift-security-context-constraints unit: tuple components/ingress-nginx (unit-enabled ingress-nginx)
okd_adaptation.depends_on_scc: openshift-security-context-constraints dep added to ingress-nginx (conditional on cabpoa)
okd_adaptation.downstream_impact: all dependent units use conditional deps; on coexistence mode they proceed with ingress-nginx
okd_adaptation.kyverno_component: okd-ingress-reencrypt added to kyverno-policies on cabpoa for re-encrypt route support
okd_adaptation.coexistence_notes: OpenShift Router stays active for Routes and OKD internals; nginx handles standard Ingress with class nginx

ingress-nginx-ready.description: checks that ingress-nginx controller is ready

nvidia-gpu-operator.notes: conditionally disables its own NFD when sriov-network-operator is enabled

ingress-nginx.notes: disabled on OKD/OCP by default; can be enabled for coexistence with OpenShift Router (requires SCC + _internal.ingressClassName override)

ingress-nginx.summary: RKE2/kubeadm uses NGINX ingress controller (rke2-ingress-nginx from Rancher charts); OKD/OCP disables it by default but can enable for coexistence with built-in Router

okd_behavior_default.tls: routes with re-encrypt via kyverno okd-ingress-reencrypt component

okd_behavior_coexistence.controller_type: DaemonSet on control-plane nodes (same as RKE2)
okd_behavior_coexistence.tls: cert-manager Certificate via sylva-ca ClusterIssuer (same as RKE2)
okd_behavior_coexistence.openshift_router: stays active for Routes and OKD internals

ingress-nginx.switch_logic: _internal.ingressClassName = cabpoa ? openshift-default : nginx (overridable in env values)

sriov.summary: same SUSE Edge operator and cattle-sriov-system namespace on all distros

sriov-crd.depends_on.reason: OKD ships multus built-in; multus-ready unit is disabled

coredns.notes: on kubeadm (cabpk) this is just a dummy to ensure upgrade from Sylva 1.3 prunes correctly

coredns-config.description: configures DNS inside cluster via CoreDNS ConfigMap patch

corefile_config.note: forwards cluster_domain queries to k8s-gateway LB IP

coredns-custom-hosts-import.description: creates ConfigMaps with workload cluster DNS records for CoreDNS import

components.okd: adds zone/upstream/hosts data for OKD workload cluster .apps domain
components.neuvector-federation: adds neuvector federation managed hostname entry

coredns-operator-config.description: patches OpenShift dns.operator/default CR to forward cluster domain queries to k8s-gateway

management_cluster_behavior.patches_dns_cr: adds server 'sylva-cluster-domain' forwarding cluster_domain to openshift.extra_virtual_ip (k8s-gateway LB)
management_cluster_behavior.clusterRoleBinding: openshift-dns-operator (grants job permission to patch DNS CR)

workload_cluster_behavior.patches_dns_cr: adds server 'management' forwarding mgmt_cluster_domain to mgmt_cluster_dns_ip
workload_cluster_behavior.purpose: allows workload cluster to resolve management cluster services (e.g. rancher, keycloak)

_coredns_okd_adaptation.summary: OKD uses the OpenShift DNS Operator to manage CoreDNS; Sylva adapts by patching the DNS operator CR instead of the ConfigMap directly

rke2_dns_flow.2_coredns_config: Kustomization patches ConfigMap with Corefile forwarding cluster_domain to k8s-gateway
rke2_dns_flow.3_kyverno_custom_hosts: ClusterPolicy watches labeled ConfigMaps, merges workload cluster host entries into CoreDNS ConfigMap, removes items restriction from volume mount, restarts Deployment
rke2_dns_flow.4_k8s_gateway: separate CoreDNS with k8s_gateway plugin resolves Ingress FQDNs to their LB IPs

okd_dns_flow.1_no_coredns_unit: coredns and coredns-config units are DISABLED (cabpoa excluded)
okd_dns_flow.2_dns_operator: OpenShift DNS Operator manages CoreDNS in openshift-dns namespace
okd_dns_flow.3_k8s_gateway: still deployed — provides external FQDN resolution via LB
okd_dns_flow.4_coredns_config_substitute: coredns-config not needed — DNS Operator handles Corefile
okd_dns_flow.5_kyverno_okd_mgmt_policy: ClusterPolicy update-dns-operator-workload-zones patches dns.operator.openshift.io/default to add forwarding zones for workload clusters pointing to k8s-gateway
okd_dns_flow.6_forwarding_zones: DNS Operator reconfigures CoreDNS to forward workload.baseDomain queries to k8s-gateway LB IP

coredns-custom-hosts-import.enabled_on: non-OKD clusters (neuvector OR cabpoa) AND NOT cabpoa mgmt
coredns-custom-hosts-import.what_it_does: patches rke2-coredns ConfigMap with workload host entries, removes items from volume mount, triggers Deployment restart

coredns-custom-hosts-import-okd-mgmt.enabled_on: OKD management clusters (cabpoa AND NOT bootstrap)
coredns-custom-hosts-import-okd-mgmt.what_it_does: patches dns.operator.openshift.io/default CR to add/remove forwarding zones for workload clusters
coredns-custom-hosts-import-okd-mgmt.on_create_update: adds spec.servers entry with zone and forwardPlugin.upstreams pointing to workload VIP
coredns-custom-hosts-import-okd-mgmt.on_delete: filters out removed entries, preserves sylva-cluster-domain and valid CMs

_coredns_okd_adaptation.key_difference: RKE2 patches CoreDNS ConfigMap directly; OKD patches the DNS Operator CR which then manages CoreDNS config
